title: Notation
description: Whispering Cave, ch.3. A symbol carved into the wall — a notation, not a language. When the Mage reads it, the wall begins to read back. Solo duel with the Prismatic Construct: rotating elemental weakness, must be read live.

steps.text: Further in, the wall had been carved. Not vandalized — *carved.* A line of symbols, evenly spaced, in a notation that wasn't quite runes and wasn't quite letters and didn't belong to any system the cave had any business knowing.

mage.text: The lead was already three feet from the wall, journal open, the pencil moving the way pencils move when they have already understood a thing and are catching up to it.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: It's a sequence. Whoever wrote it knew about the cave before the cave knew about itself.

default.text: The Mage was already at the wall — eyes moving left to right and then right to left and then left to right again, the way you read a thing that doesn't agree with itself.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: It's not a language. It's a notation. There's a difference.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Languages tell you what something *means.* Notations tell you what something *is.* This one says: the cave was measured. Recently. By someone who already knew the answer.

steps.text: The symbols lifted, one at a time, off the stone. They arranged themselves in the air — a construct of geometric light, hexagonal at first, then radial, then cycling. Its color kept changing. So did what its color meant.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Everyone stand back. This one is a reading problem. I'll handle it.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Watch the color. Whatever it is, the answer is the other one.

steps.text: The Construct came apart the way it had come together — one symbol at a time, back onto the stone. When the last one settled, the wall was, again, just a wall. Or almost. The Mage saw it wasn't.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: No — wait. The third symbol. I had it as a constant. It's a *variable*.

lines.speaker: Mage
lines.text: Then it's the other thing. Someone wasn't measuring the cave. Someone was *defining* it.

steps.text: He did not flinch when he revised himself. He did not apologize for the first reading. He simply wrote the second one underneath, and the first one was a step on the way to the second one, and both were in the journal.
The Mage was, the party would learn, the kind of person who would rather have the wrong question written correctly than the right question forgotten.
